data.narrative:
Pixxel is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $95M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M)—alongside 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median P/S multiple of 42.9x.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M total raised as a proxy base and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.9x, yields $180M. Peer-median analysis across the 3 same-stage, same-sector private comparables produces $1.3B, with valuations spanning $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Pixxel's revenue is not disclosed, precluding a P/S calculation despite the 42.9x sector median.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $153M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $740M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around growth trajectory and commercial traction relative to peers. Stage-progression implies a more conservative floor, while peer-median suggests substantially higher value positioning Pixxel within the upper quartile of Series B aerospace companies. Resolution of revenue scale, customer contracts, and technology validation would narrow this range materially.
